purpose: Build a deterministic-first repair path for Ananta that prefers structured diagnostics, known failure signatures, verified repair procedures and success memory over unnecessary LLM calls, while still allowing bounded LLM escalation for unknown or ambiguous situations.
goal: Ananta should diagnose and repair common local system and development environment problems through structured, repeatable and increasingly evidence-based flows, using LLM reasoning only when deterministic knowledge and bounded procedural logic are insufficient.
success_criteria: Known repair cases can be recognized through signatures without requiring LLM interpretation.
Deterministic diagnosis and repair flows exist for a useful subset of high-value failure classes.
The system records which fixes actually succeeded, failed or were unsafe in real contexts.
Each repair step is verified explicitly before the next step is attempted.
LLM escalation happens only when deterministic paths are insufficient or confidence is too low.
Repair behavior remains auditable, bounded and approval-aware.

milestones.title: Foundation and signal ingestion
milestones.target_outcome: Repair target/state/confidence models and bounded evidence ingestion are defined.

milestones.title: Signatures and diagnosis
milestones.target_outcome: Deterministic signature matching and structured diagnosis runners/playbooks exist.

milestones.title: Repair and verification execution
milestones.target_outcome: Bounded repair execution and per-step/final verification are integrated.

milestones.title: Memory, escalation and governance
milestones.target_outcome: Outcome memory, controlled LLM escalation and hard safety/approval rails are in place.

milestones.title: Operator enablement and rollout
milestones.target_outcome: Operator views, tests, docs and phased rollout are complete.

tasks.title: Repair path target model define
acceptance_criteria: A target model defines deterministic-first repair behavior for Ananta.
The model distinguishes diagnosis, repair proposal, repair execution and verification.
The model explicitly defines when the system may escalate to an LLM.

tasks.title: Repair problem class inventory create
acceptance_criteria: A first inventory exists for high-value repair classes such as package install failures, service start failures, port conflicts, path issues, permission issues, compose failures and runtime health failures.
Each class has examples, signals and likely safe action ranges.
The inventory is realistic for Ananta's intended local/admin use.

tasks.title: Repair state model define
acceptance_criteria: A state model exists for repair sessions such as detected, diagnosing, proposing, approval_required, executing, verifying, succeeded, failed and escalated.
State transitions are explicit and support auditability.
The model fits later KRITIS and approval work.

tasks.title: Repair confidence model define
acceptance_criteria: A confidence model defines when the system trusts deterministic matches, when it asks for review and when it escalates to LLM reasoning.
Confidence can combine signature strength, platform match and past fix success.
The model is explainable.

tasks.title: Repair evidence ingestion model define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines which evidence types can enter a repair session such as logs, exit codes, health checks, config snippets, service status and environment facts.
Evidence classes are normalized enough for deterministic matching.
The design remains bounded and privacy-aware.

tasks.title: Environment fact collection add
acceptance_criteria: Repair sessions can collect structured environment facts such as OS, distro, package manager, runtime versions, container state and relevant service state.
Fact collection is bounded and reproducible.
The fact set is usable for platform-specific matching.

tasks.title: Structured log ingestion add
acceptance_criteria: The repair path can ingest logs from selected sources in a structured way.
The ingestion preserves source provenance, timestamps and severity where available.
The result supports later signature matching.

tasks.title: Health and command result capture add
acceptance_criteria: Repair sessions can store relevant command outputs, exit codes and health-check results in a normalized form.
These results are linked to the current repair session.
The captured data is available to deterministic rules.

tasks.title: Evidence normalization pipeline implement
acceptance_criteria: Ingested evidence is normalized into a stable internal representation.
The normalization supports pattern matching and history comparison.
Noise reduction does not destroy important diagnostic detail.

tasks.title: Failure signature model define
acceptance_criteria: A formal model exists for failure signatures.
Signatures can include regex patterns, structured fields, environment constraints and confidence weights.
The model can express platform- or tool-specific failures.

tasks.title: Initial signature catalog create
acceptance_criteria: A first catalog exists for common failures in Windows, Ubuntu or common development stacks relevant to Ananta.
The catalog includes enough metadata to drive diagnosis and repair selection.
The first catalog focuses on high-value and reasonably safe cases.

tasks.title: Signature matching engine implement
acceptance_criteria: A matching engine can score ingested evidence against known failure signatures.
Multiple matches can be ranked and explained.
The engine does not require LLM calls for normal matching.

tasks.title: Ambiguous and low-confidence match handling add
acceptance_criteria: The system can detect ambiguity or weak matches.
Low-confidence cases lead to bounded next steps instead of blind repair.
The behavior is consistent with the repair confidence model.

tasks.title: Signature explanation output add
acceptance_criteria: The system can explain which signature matched and why.
The explanation includes key evidence and platform constraints.
The output is short and useful for operator trust.

tasks.title: Diagnosis procedure model define
acceptance_criteria: A procedure model exists for deterministic diagnosis flows.
Procedures can encode ordered checks, branching checks and stop conditions.
The model supports verification checkpoints.

tasks.title: Initial diagnosis playbooks create
acceptance_criteria: A first set of deterministic diagnosis playbooks exists for the highest-value repair classes.
Playbooks are structured and not just prose notes.
Each playbook includes safe stop conditions.

tasks.title: Diagnosis runner implement
acceptance_criteria: The system can execute diagnosis playbooks step by step.
Each step can collect new evidence or classify the case further.
The runner produces structured state updates.

tasks.title: Diagnosis branching and fallback behavior add
acceptance_criteria: Diagnosis procedures can branch based on observed evidence.
Fallback behavior is explicit and not hidden.
The runner can stop early when sufficient evidence exists.

tasks.title: Non-destructive first strategy enforce
acceptance_criteria: Diagnosis defaults to non-destructive checks before proposing mutation.
Destructive or state-changing steps are never treated as normal diagnosis.
The strategy is enforced, not just documented.

tasks.title: Repair procedure model define
acceptance_criteria: A repair procedure model exists for executable fixes.
The model includes preconditions, steps, postconditions, verification and rollback hints where possible.
The model can mark procedures as safe, review-first or high-risk.

tasks.title: Initial deterministic repair catalog create
acceptance_criteria: A first catalog of deterministic repair procedures exists for common failure classes.
Each procedure is tied to specific signatures or diagnosis outcomes.
The catalog is bounded to well-understood repairs rather than speculative fixes.

tasks.title: Repair step executor implement
acceptance_criteria: Repair procedures can be executed through a bounded step executor.
Execution is integrated with approval and safety policy layers.
Each executed step is recorded and verifiable.

tasks.title: Dry-run and preview support add
acceptance_criteria: Repair procedures support dry-run or preview where meaningful.
Users can inspect intended actions before execution.
The preview is explicit about limitations.

tasks.title: Safe stop and abort conditions add
acceptance_criteria: Repair execution can stop cleanly when conditions worsen or become unsafe.
Abort conditions are explicit and logged.
The system avoids blindly continuing after contradictory evidence.

tasks.title: Verification model define
acceptance_criteria: A verification model exists for repair procedures and diagnosis milestones.
Verification can use health checks, service status, command outputs or functional probes.
Verification is mandatory for state-changing repair actions.

tasks.title: Per-step verification hooks add
acceptance_criteria: Each important repair step can trigger specific verification checks.
Verification results decide whether to continue, stop or roll back.
The model avoids fire-and-forget repair execution.

tasks.title: Final repair success verification add
acceptance_criteria: Every repair flow ends with an explicit success or failure verification.
The final result is based on evidence, not just command completion.
The result updates repair history.

tasks.title: Rollback or recovery hint model add
acceptance_criteria: Where full rollback is not possible, the system can at least record recovery hints or manual undo guidance.
Recovery information is linked to the repair procedure.
The feature improves operator safety.

tasks.title: Repair outcome memory model define
acceptance_criteria: A structured model exists for storing repair attempts and outcomes.
The model records signature, environment facts, chosen procedure, result and verification evidence.
The model is queryable later.

tasks.title: Fix success and failure tracking add
acceptance_criteria: The system records whether a fix succeeded, partially helped, failed or made things worse.
Outcome labels are explicit and standardized.
The tracking supports future recommendation scoring.

tasks.title: Environment similarity model add
acceptance_criteria: Repair history can reason about similarity across environment facts such as OS, version, package manager and service context.
The model helps reuse successful procedures in similar contexts.
The logic remains explainable.

tasks.title: Success-weighted repair recommendation add
acceptance_criteria: The system can rank repair procedures partly by past success in similar cases.
Ranking does not override hard safety rules.
The recommendation explains both signature match and historical confidence.

tasks.title: Negative learning and anti-pattern recording add
acceptance_criteria: The system can remember procedures or steps that repeatedly fail or worsen a class of issue.
Negative history can reduce or block certain suggestions later.
The feature supports safer long-term behavior.

tasks.title: LLM escalation policy define
acceptance_criteria: A policy defines when LLM escalation is allowed such as unknown signatures, contradictory evidence or exhausted deterministic paths.
The policy explicitly prevents unnecessary LLM use in known cases.
Escalation reasons are auditable.

tasks.title: Bounded repair escalation prompt model add
acceptance_criteria: When escalation occurs, the LLM receives a bounded structured repair context instead of raw uncontrolled logs only.
The prompt includes known evidence, attempted paths and confidence information.
The model supports safer and more targeted LLM output.

tasks.title: LLM proposal-to-procedure conversion path add
acceptance_criteria: An LLM-suggested fix can be converted into a reviewed or structured repair proposal instead of being executed blindly.
The path favors explicit procedure generation and review.
The feature does not bypass approval.

tasks.title: Escalation feedback loop into repair knowledge add
acceptance_criteria: Useful escalated outcomes can later be curated into deterministic signatures or procedures.
The system supports moving repeated LLM-learned cases into structured knowledge.
The curation step remains explicit.

tasks.title: Repair action safety classes define
acceptance_criteria: Repair actions are classified into safety classes such as inspect-only, bounded low-risk, confirm-required and high-risk.
The classes are enforced consistently.
The classification integrates with broader approval logic.

tasks.title: Approval integration for repair procedures add
acceptance_criteria: Repair steps that require approval cannot execute without it.
Approval scope is tied to procedure, target and session where appropriate.
The integration is backend-enforced.

tasks.title: Repair audit chain add
acceptance_criteria: Repair sessions produce a traceable audit chain from diagnosis through execution and verification.
Audit records show whether deterministic logic or LLM escalation was used.
The chain supports later incident review.

tasks.title: Unsafe action guardrails add
acceptance_criteria: The system has explicit guardrails for obviously dangerous or out-of-scope repair actions.
Guardrails fail closed where appropriate.
The behavior is visible and testable.

tasks.title: Repair session summary view add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can see a concise summary of a repair session.
The summary includes current state, detected signature, chosen path and verification status.
The presentation is useful in UI or TUI.

tasks.title: Deterministic versus escalated path visibility add
acceptance_criteria: Users can tell whether the current repair path is deterministic, mixed or LLM-escalated.
The visibility helps trust and review.
The presentation is simple.

tasks.title: Repair proposal preview UX add
acceptance_criteria: Before mutation-capable repair actions, users can inspect the proposed steps and expected verification.
The preview is good enough for approval decisions.
The UX remains compact.

tasks.title: Repair history inspection view add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can inspect prior repair outcomes and reuse evidence.
History can be filtered by issue class or environment.
The view is practical for recurring problems.

tasks.title: Signature matching tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify deterministic signature matching for representative failure classes.
Ambiguous and no-match cases are covered.
Regression risk is reduced.

tasks.title: Diagnosis and repair procedure tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover representative diagnosis and repair flows.
Verification and approval gating are included in the suite.
The suite protects the deterministic-first model.

tasks.title: Repair memory and ranking tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify success recording, failure recording and recommendation ranking behavior.
The suite covers similar-environment reuse at least minimally.
The behavior stays predictable.

tasks.title: Operator guide for deterministic repair path write
acceptance_criteria: Documentation explains how deterministic repair works, when approval is needed and when LLM escalation occurs.
The guide includes examples and safety notes.
The guide is useful for local admin scenarios.

tasks.title: Golden path examples for common repair classes add
acceptance_criteria: At least a few reproducible repair golden paths exist for common classes such as service start failure or package issue.
Each example shows diagnosis, proposal, verification and result recording.
The examples demonstrate the deterministic-first value.

tasks.title: Rollout plan define
acceptance_criteria: A rollout plan exists that introduces deterministic repair in phases rather than all at once.
The plan identifies the first supported repair classes.
The rollout remains manageable.

summary_notes: Deterministic repair foundations, evidence ingestion and first signature modeling wave (REP-T01 to REP-T10) are implemented.
Milestone REP-M1 is completed and REP-M2 is started, while the remaining execution track stays aligned to execution_order_summary and critical_path_tasks.
Risk-heavy remaining work is concentrated in signature matching engine depth, repair execution, governance enforcement and deterministic verification.
